children.heading: Identification and inspection of facilities
children.label: a Identification and inspection of facilities
A
children.text: The known safety risks of the food manufactured, processed, packed, or held at the facility.

children.label: B
children.text: The compliance history of a facility, including with regard to food recalls, outbreaks of foodborne illness, and violations of food safety standards.

children.label: C
children.text: The rigor and effectiveness of the facility’s hazard analysis and risk-based preventive controls.

children.label: D
children.text: Whether the food manufactured, processed, packed, or held at the facility meets the criteria for priority under .section 381(h)(1) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Whether the food or the facility that manufactured, processed, packed, or held such food has received a certification as described in section 381(q) or 384b of this title, as appropriate.

children.label: F
children.text: Any other criteria deemed necessary and appropriate by the Secretary for purposes of allocating inspection resources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning on , the Secretary shall increase the frequency of inspection of all facilities.January 4, 2011

children.label: i
children.text: not less often than once in the 5-year period following ; andJanuary 4, 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: not less often than once every 3 years thereafter.

children.label: i
children.text: not less often than once in the 7-year period following ; andJanuary 4, 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: not less often than once every 5 years thereafter.

children.heading: Year 1
children.label: i Year 1
children.text: In the 1-year period following , the Secretary shall inspect not fewer than 600 foreign facilities.January 4, 2011

children.heading: Subsequent years
children.label: ii Subsequent years
children.text: In each of the 5 years following the 1-year period described in clause (i), the Secretary shall inspect not fewer than twice the number of foreign facilities inspected by the Secretary during the previous year.

children.heading: Reliance on Federal, State, or local inspections
children.label: E Reliance on Federal, State, or local inspections
children.text: In meeting the inspection requirements under this subsection for domestic facilities, the Secretary may rely on inspections conducted by other Federal, State, or local agencies under interagency agreement, contract, memoranda of understanding, or other obligation.

children.heading: Identification and inspection at ports of entry
children.label: b Identification and inspection at ports of entry

children.text: The known safety risks of the food imported.
The known safety risks of the countries or regions of origin and countries through which such article of food is transported.
The compliance history of the importer, including with regard to food recalls, outbreaks of foodborne illness, and violations of food safety standards.

The rigor and effectiveness of the activities conducted by the importer of such article of food to satisfy the requirements of the foreign supplier verification program under .section 384a of this title
Whether the food importer participates in the voluntary qualified importer program under .section 384b of this title
Whether the food meets the criteria for priority under .section 381(h)(1) of this title
Whether the food or the facility that manufactured, processed, packed, or held such food received a certification as described in section 381(q) or 384b of this title.
Any other criteria deemed necessary and appropriate by the Secretary for purposes of allocating inspection resources.

children.heading: Interagency agreements with respect to seafood
children.label: c Interagency agreements with respect to seafood

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Chairman of the Federal Trade Commission, and the heads of other appropriate agencies may enter into such agreements as may be necessary or appropriate to improve seafood safety.

children.label: A
children.text: cooperative arrangements for examining and testing seafood imports that leverage the resources, capabilities, and authorities of each party to the agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: coordination of inspections of foreign facilities to increase the percentage of imported seafood and seafood facilities inspected;

children.label: C
children.text: standardization of data on seafood names, inspection records, and laboratory testing to improve interagency coordination;

children.label: D
children.text: coordination to detect and investigate violations under applicable Federal law;

children.label: E
children.text: a process, including the use or modification of existing processes, by which officers and employees of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration may be duly designated by the Secretary to carry out seafood examinations and investigations under or section 203 of the Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004;section 381 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the sharing of information concerning observed non-compliance with United States food requirements domestically and in foreign nations and new regulatory decisions and policies that may affect the safety of food imported into the United States;

children.label: G
children.text: conducting joint training on subjects that affect and strengthen seafood inspection effectiveness by Federal authorities; and

children.label: H
children.text: outreach on Federal efforts to enhance seafood safety and compliance with Federal food safety requirements.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: d Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall improve coordination and cooperation with the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Homeland Security to target food inspection resources.

children.heading: Facility
children.label: e Facility
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “facility” means a domestic facility or a foreign facility that is required to register under .section 350d of this title
